derivations.answer:
The three possible values for the 'loadPlugins' property within a CliCommandPathPolicy are:

1. **"always"** - Plugins are always loaded regardless of output mode. This setting ensures full plugin functionality is available during command execution.

2. **"text-only"** - Plugins are loaded only when the output is not in JSON mode. When the CLI outputs text (standard operation), plugins are loaded; when outputting JSON, plugins are skipped to improve performance or reduce complexity.

3. **"never"** - Plugins are never loaded during command execution. This minimizes startup overhead and limits available commands to those built into the core application without plugin extensions.

Each setting directly controls whether plugins are initialized before executing specific CLI commands, affecting feature availability based on the command path policy configured in the command catalog.

The three possible values for the 'loadPlugins' property within CliCommandPathPolicy are:

1. **"never"** - Plugins are never loaded during command execution, meaning any subcommands provided by plugins will not be available to the user. This is typically the default policy for most commands (see line 127 in src/cli/command-catalog.ts).

2. **"always"** - All plugins are loaded during command execution, making all plugin-provided subcommands available regardless of output mode. Commands like "agent", "message", "channels", "directory", "agents", and "configure" use this policy (lines 33-38 in src/cli/command-catalog.ts).

3. **"text-only"** - Plugins are loaded unless the output mode is JSON. When text output is used, all plugin subcommands are available; when using the "--json" flag, plugins are not loaded. The "status" command uses this policy with special handling (line 42 in src/cli/command-catalog.ts).

The availability of subcommands is affected because the CLI framework only registers subcommands from plugins that are loaded during startup. If plugins aren't loaded ("never" policy), those subcommands won't exist in the command catalog.
